description: The platform has helped us a lot, we have created around 3 different flows and it has never given us any inconvenience that could not be fixed with some changes in user permissions, so I give my recommendation of the platform 100% Apart from this, the platform allows you to incorporate an unlimited number of users and the fact that the platform is designed in a web environment, which allows you to have access to the tool from any device and in case there is little, it has a mobile application. which allows you to have immediate and available access at any time
author: Diego C.
pros: the use of the application is quite easy, knowledge of it is not necessary for the administration of the application, approvals and flows are quite friendly, and the best thing about the tool is the cost-benefit, process maker has come to facilitate our work on a daily basis, we use it to create an approval of the flow of purchases at a regional level and it has been very effective in addition to the report module is very accurate and allows you to export the information either in excel or work it in PowerBI
cons: The only thing I can say that the application is bad is the customer service, the technical support is very late as the salespeople, we reached a point where we almost suspended the license for not having an offer for the renewal of In the same way, although the license price is very accessible, it is necessary to buy a license for each user and also for the flow created.

description: Overall, i was satisfied with the level of productivity i got from using this software alone. Especially in a small/medium company. I have to say that I really did not experience any unforeseen crashes, the software also responds quite well to changes ( except upgrades, it was terrible), the workflows have to be checked after each upgrade because sometimes, they break.
author: Nothando M.
pros: I used ProcessMaker for a year but in 2 different companies and under 2 different licences and the following features were the most appealing to me. 1. The layout was familiar from the word go - ProcessMaker utilizes an almost email like generic layout which made it easy for me to understand it, use it and implement it. There is a section for drafts and even one for inbox. 2. Allows easy tracking and workflow management because of how the folders are split out hence a positive addition for the business as a whole - you are able to view due dates at first glance, see prioritized tasks and have a real time snapshot of where your project is.. 3. It is not the hardest tool to use, in fact it's actually quite easy - ProcesMAker actually utilizes the drag and drop feature that is a very familiar feature as computer OS systems also have it, using it for the first time does not feel like it's the first time. 4. Since it's a cloud software, you can access all your data on several devices and different locations as long as you have a connection. This is very convenient. I also noticed that sometimes, I did not have to be logged into the company network to access my data. 5. It works well, smoothly and feels good to use.
cons: I think the license is a bit expensive and if the company is big then this is a revenue drain. The problem is that the license is on a per user basis. A product that requires so many licenses ultimately slows down the business, spare licenses would help alleviate this problem but I have not seen the initiative yet. Performance of individual users within the software is also greatly affected by how hard it is to create reports, a user needs to take time and understand the software before he/she can perform reports. The user interface, even though familiar , feels a little bit outdated and could use an upgrade or a make-over. Lastly, when you run into issues, customer support is not the best, could be improved. Broken workflows area a lot to fix and usually happen after a system upgrade, this could be improved.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall experience. Okay, I would like to say about their support. Their support is fantastic and very quick. But the problem I faced in migration. Even they support for migration also but from different version to other version data migration is not possible. Processmaker should develop one stable version like 3.0.1.8 and also provide data migration from lowest version to the newest version.
author: Salman M.
pros: This software is good where the user or company only needs to computerized their paperwork. Processmaker should add the capabilities for CRM -> Projects, Leads, Quotations, etc.
cons: Every feature Processmaker includes is good and I almost use all features. All features are important and depend on workflow requirement. In some workflows, email feature is a very important mean while other workflows have other features that are important than email.

review_title: Good for end user, Bad for reporting and case tracking
description: Migrating the different process in the company about financial and open cases. For example, the process for request access to IT, is in ProcessMaker, and reduce the time for response and the user can track the ticket approved
author: Douglas O.
pros: Easy for use, principal for the end users and Administrators. The easy development the different process in the company. ProcessMaker help us to safe the planet, migrating the paper to digital forms
cons: The reports, is more easy create reports in other program compare with the reporting environment in the system and the part of case tracking is very dificult.

review_title: Feature rich and easy to user BPMS
author: Kostas P.
pros: Fully fledged BPM platform with complete toolset for defining processes, designing forms and integrating with external systems. Well documented APIs (both JavaScript and PHP) and easy to use user portal.
cons: Even though user portal is fully functional, UI/UX could be improved by incorporating modern SPA technologies.

author: Ethan P.
pros: ProcessMaker allows you to customize most aspects of your processes including the forms, the underlying logic, and email notifications. If you are asked to create a complicated system, it can probably keep up with you.
cons: When we tried to use many of the features we discovered that there were bugs in those features. When I first setup a process, I had about 10 confirmed bugs in their bug tracker. It takes 6-12 months for bugs to get fixed in my experience and every time they release a minor update it fixes one of the bugs and adds a new one. For example, the search feature has been broken for several months now, so users have to use the reporting module to search for past cases. It runs much slower than the built in search.

review_title: Feedback on purchase and installatio of ProcessMaker BPM Software
description: Experience is good so far but requires training of users which could have come helpful with license purchase - additional approval to fund training of users is being sought to bridge this gap.
author: Pius E.
pros: Contracting process and support for onboarding Easy of setup
cons: Scarce local support Training credits not available with purchase as with other solution providers such as IBM, Microsoft etc.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Artemio E.
pros: We start with version 3.x, easy to implement
cons: Version 4.x had a paradigm shift, which makes deployment and development difficult

description: Started to used for quality process, connecting franchises and vendors to validate changes.. it was really usefil as we could engage several stakeholders that before had a very manual process.. process timings wer reduced from 30 days to less than 2 The interface is very web basic, but it has helped us significantly in our digitalization and process oriented journey
author: José Antonio B.
pros: Very open BPM platform, can be connected to multiple platforms
cons: Usability, ease of use is reasonable but a little updated

description: Overall a good product for its productivity and highly trained support staff. User interface for administration and users is easy to learn.
author: Muhammad Adil M.
pros: For integration workflows it is the best tool in market. Quick and fast working model and you can have finished your tasks in a jiffy. Documentation is complete and elaborative for PHP and JavaScript.
cons: Debugging is bit tricky and not so good integrated module for third party. Local training is not available like other products in market

description: 1. Excellent experience overall. 2. The workflow software can be used for real time, quick turnaround time, operational transactions. 3. If designed well, the workflow software can even replace an ERP system.
author: Satya Gopal K.
pros: 1. It is standardized, yet flexible. 2. It has many features that can compete with normal software development. 3. Workflows can be developed quickly. 4. It has a community version that allows a prospective user to try out various features before committing to purchase the commercial version. 5. The technical support team is very responsive. 6. Very reliable architecture. Does not crash.
cons: 1. It is not a workflow software that can be developed by end users. There are some others that can be done that way. 2. The more skilled one is at programming, esp. PHP and JavaScript, the better the usage of ProcessMaker's features.

description: We have managed to create workflows and approval for the optimization of times and responses of our company and our IT department. In general, the tool meets our expectations and satisfies the needs of our CIO and other employees.
author: José V.
pros: I like how to optimize processes, in our company we use it for purchasing processes, we have created flows with our process architect that help in the approval of purchase orders.
cons: What hinders us is to depend on an architect for the generation of the flows, it would be excellent if modifications can be made at the application level without needing the accompaniment of a specialist. For the rest it is a very good tool.

review_title: The best bpm on the market
description: With processmaker we implemented some solutions for the municipal administration and we had a success, we managed the pqrd of the city and the platform responded in all aspects, the technical support has been made using this tool, we have been able to adjust the requested changes for the client
author: Lennin Geovanny G.
pros: It is easy to install, has a good documentation online, allows you to install a free version to test it before buying, streamlines the development and implementation of solutions, safe, reliable, friendly, generates reports agile and easy
cons: migration from version 2.8 to 3.x is complex, in version 3 several features were removed that were previously free and are now part of paid plugins, which does not allow testing before you can decide whether to buy or not

review_title: Looking to streamline your business? Look no more.
description: Processmaker is a rock solid tool if you know how to use it and understand the value it can deliver. To get the best value design processes with your team that understands the goals of your organization.
author: Basil A.
pros: Ease of use.
cons: None. But if you are not with an IT background you will need help in understanding the workflow and how to use the tool.

review_title: My Experience with Process Maker Software
description: The solution has saved cost for my organization and has improved our turn around time.
author: Henry O.
pros: The software makes manual processes faster. The software can be customized to suit business need and it enhancement on already built processes is very easy.
cons: The fact that some codes written in old versions of the application will need to almost be written again upon upgrade to a new version.

review_title: Consider to user BPM Processmaker because it is open source
description: powerful software for business process management
author: Budi B.
pros: - Open source is one of our benefit - Interface simple but user friendly - Workflows defined is easy - Source can be modify because its open source - Email is easy to configure - User management have complete information - documentation good
cons: - Output as PDF format need to fixed out - License price is too expensive - should need experience in javascript because using many parameter for approval or workflow process - support only come from community

review_title: Evolving platform that has ongoing potential
description: Powerful tool that does require good knowledge of JavaScript, PHP and MySQL however as someone with just learning these codes I have implemented a number of processes. The support team are fantastic and will help you through coding issues. Feature requests are implemented quickly and overall we have seem an improvement internally with our processes. ProcessMaker are very receptive to feedback and appear to genuinely welcome feedback requests to improve their software. I put 3 stars for ease of use as I don't know PHP, JavaScript or MySQL but am learning as I go. Overall compared to other vendors I investigated, for the price point this is a very good tool.
author: Lee N.
pros: Flexibility in process design, great support, good documentation, powerful tool, Mobile app
cons: Output to PDF function is fairly basic in terms of layout and design.

author: Gabriele B.
pros: Easy of use by users that do not have real development skills
cons: Integration with External system is sometimes challenging

description: I developed some <BPM projects with ProcessMaker, its amazing because ProcessMaker its full oriented to enduser with BPMN 2.0
author: Pedro M.
pros: Objective and oriented to enduser like as email system
cons: Dashboard component needs to evaluate more

review_title: Very logical and contains almost all features we need. Handy tool.
author: Laszlo V.
pros: - User friendly BPMN designer; - Output document management possibility; - Nice usage of PHP triggers; - Customizable user interface (columns, case notes, csae descriptions, ...)
cons: - Document management and searching capabilities are poor; - Dynaform formatting is a bit complicated, previous versions gave more support.

description: Processmaker software has the capability to solve business workflow at ease and its capability and ease of use among other bpm software with further expansion as open source is unique.
author: Patrick T.
pros: Right price and performance especially able to perform multi-level workflow
cons: Lack of other integrations or plugin to a common platform like wordpress, google and other database.

author: Adebimpe A.
pros: The software is very easy to use, as the workflow is highly simplified
cons: The graphics quality and monotonous methods

review_title: Awesome, using pm for critical it processes
author: Juan Z.
pros: GUI is the most that I like, is so easy to use and present for common users so the visualization allows you to deploy processes
cons: The community is not so strong sometime you have to look for explanations and ways to solve issues and documentation or community support is low

description: The overall experience was very positive and with a bit more time and actual usage of this product may be one of the programs we may upgrade to in the future if not cost prohibitive.
author: Ann R.
pros: We tried this product on a trial basis as we are comparing it to other workflow products we currently use to see if it has better and easier capabilities.
cons: We did note that ProcessMaker Workflow does offer a bit more accessible and easier methods of establishing the workflows than what we are currently using. ProcessMaker Workflow does seem to be a bit more intuitive.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: best php written workflowengine on the market
description: i have searched for a long time and i must say, that processmaker fits my needs on a standalone workflowengine best. i love the simple plugin mechanism an the flexibility of the triggers. So i was able to integrate 3rd party soap apps ver easyly with the new designer, we can even win pitches agains well known java applications. Great work on your 3.0 In only 2 days we have implemented a showcase with a heavy weight magento-store on a monster Adobe Indesign Backend. What a great fun to work with. The very good documentation helped me like a charm. Processmaker is the perfect base to integrate workflows in webapplications and its your best friend if you speak php. I hope for the future, that the backend code will be a little more modern and Zend Framework or Symfony or Cake will find the way in the very cool product. All the best Peter
author: Peter B.
